$comment: Single source of truth for ALL branding. Edit this, run `pnpm brand:apply`, and every consumer (Capacitor configs, electron-builder, Info.plist, entitlements, web manifest, Swift constants, root package.json, README badges) is regenerated. Adding a new consumer? Add a function to scripts/native/apply-brand.mjs.
name: career-ops
tagline: Run your job search like a product launch.
description: Career Ops is an AI-powered job search platform: multi-user dashboard, autonomous apply across 11 ATS portals, tailored CVs and cover letters per role, interview prep, follow-up cadence, and offline-first native apps on macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, iPadOS, Android, and Apple Watch.

splash.$comment: Used by native/icons/generate-icons.mjs to fill the iOS Splash.imageset PNG canvas. Should match capacitor.config.ts → plugins.SplashScreen.backgroundColor so the launch-screen storyboard and the @capacitor/splash-screen overlay look identical.

originalAuthor.name: santifer
originalAuthor.comment: Original creator of the career-ops project — this fork builds on their work. Credited in docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md and README upstream link. Do not remove.

permissions.localNetworkUsage: Career Ops uses local network discovery to find your desktop app on the same Wi-Fi network so your phone, watch, and laptop stay in sync without a cloud account.
permissions.faceIdUsage: Career Ops uses Face ID to unlock sensitive screens — interview notes, negotiation playbooks, and offer comparisons — so a glance over your shoulder never leaks personal data.
permissions.cameraUsage: Career Ops uses the camera so you can scan business cards from interviews directly into your contacts file.
permissions.microphoneUsage: Career Ops uses the microphone for the mock-interview rehearsal mode — audio is processed on-device and never uploaded.
permissions.photoLibraryUsage: Career Ops needs photo-library access to attach screenshots of job postings or reference letters to a job entry.
permissions.calendarsUsage: Career Ops reads your calendar to surface upcoming interviews and to schedule follow-up reminders.
permissions.remindersUsage: Career Ops creates reminders for follow-ups, application deadlines, and interview prep blocks.
permissions.contactsUsage: Career Ops reads contacts so you can send outreach to recruiters and hiring managers you already know.
permissions.userTrackingUsage: Career Ops never tracks you. This prompt only appears because Apple requires it for any app that can render web content — we always respond with denied.

copyright: © 2026 resist.js (original work © 2026 santifer). Licensed under the MIT License.

appStore.subtitle: AI job-search command center
appStore.reviewerNotes: Career Ops is a personal job-search productivity tool. The app reads job postings the user pastes, drafts tailored CVs/cover letters locally, and tracks application status in a local SQLite database. No personal data leaves the device unless the user explicitly signs in with their own LLM API key (Anthropic / OpenAI / Google). Demo account: not applicable — the app is single-user / self-hosted by design.
appStore.promotionalText: Run your job search like a product launch — local-first, multi-platform, AI-tailored.
appStore.marketingText: Tailor CVs, scan portals, track applications, prep interviews. All in one offline-first dashboard.

playStore.shortDescription: AI-powered job-search command center. Local-first, offline, private.
playStore.fullDescription: Career Ops is a personal job-search productivity tool. Tailor CVs and cover letters per role with on-device LLMs, scan 11+ job portals (LinkedIn, Greenhouse, Ashby, Lever, Workday, …), track every application in a local SQLite database, and prep interviews with mock-question + STAR-story drills. Data stays on your device unless you sign in with your own LLM API key. Open-source, MIT-licensed.
